FDP_ACC.1: Subset access control - restricts access to user data to authorized subjects.
FDP_ACF.1: Security attribute based access control - enforces access based on security attributes.
FDP_SDC.1: Subset data confidentiality - protects a defined subset of data from disclosure.
FDP_SDI.1: Stored data integrity monitoring - detects unauthorized modification of stored data.
FDP_UCT.1: Data transfer confidentiality - protects data from disclosure during transmission.
FDP_UID.1: User data isolation - separates data belonging to different users.
FIA_UAU.1: Timing of authentication - defines when users must be authenticated.
FIA_UAU.2: User authentication before any action - requires authentication prior to use.
FIA_UID.1: User identification - uniquely identifies users before authentication.
FMT_LIM.2: Limited capabilities - restricts users to a defined set of security-relevant operations.
FMT_MOF.1: Management of security functions behavior - controls how TSF functions behave.
FMT_MSA.1: Management of security attributes - controls security attributes of subjects/objects.
FMT_MTD.1: Management of TSF data - controls management of trusted security function data.
FPR_ANO.1: Anonymity - protects user identity from other users.
FPR_PSE.1: Pseudonymity - ensures a user can use a resource without revealing identity.
FPR_UNL.1: Unlinkability - prevents linkage of operations to the same user.
FPT_ITA.1: Availability of exported TSF data - protects TSF data availability when exported.
FPT_ITC.1: Confidentiality of exported TSF data - protects confidentiality of TSF data when exported.
FPT_ITI.1: Integrity of TSF data - protects TSF data from unauthorized modification.
FPT_PHP.1: Passive detection of physical attack - detects physical tampering.
FPT_PHP.2: Active detection of physical attack - actively responds to physical tampering.
FPT_PHP.3: Resistance to physical attack - resists physical probing and manipulation.
FPT_RPL.1: Replay detection - detects and rejects replayed data or commands.
FPT_STM.1: TSF self-testing - verifies correct operation of the security functions.
FRU_ELT.1: Error logging - records errors that may affect security.
FRU_FLT.1: Degraded fault tolerance - maintains defined security in presence of failures.
FRU_PRS.1: Limited service priority - prioritizes security-relevant functions under stress.
FRU_RSA.1: Robustness of service availability - maintains availability against defined attacks.
